title: calophyllum andersonii
intro: Calophyllum andersonii, also known as the 'Sarangan' or 'Andaman Sandalwood', is a species of tree in the Calophyllaceae family, native to the Andaman and Nicobar Islands in India. This plant is rich in medicinal properties, primarily due to its bark, which contains a high concentration of calophyllolide, a compound that has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions. The medicinal properties of Calophyllum andersonii have been found to have anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal effects, which can help to alleviate symptoms of fever, cough, and skin infections, while also providing relief from pain and inflammation. In traditional herbal preparations, the bark of Calophyllum andersonii is often used to make teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are used to treat a range of health issues, including respiratory problems and skin conditions.
